column10: A gecko is a small lizard found often in Asian countries. It has sticky feet so they can hang on walls and doorframes. If a predator like a cat chases them and tries to tap them, their tail can fall off and they can run to safety. There are more than 12 different kinds of geckos in Singapore. They eat insects so there are not too many of them in your house. There are many different colors and kinds of geckos.

column10: A great white shark is a large fish that lives in the ocean. It's known for its powerful body and sharp teeth. These sharks can grow very big, some can be as long as a school bus! Great whites are part of the shark family and only live in water. These sharks are meat eaters (carnivores) and very protective of their home (territorial).
column12: We do not know exactly how long great white sharks have been around, but scientists have found fossils dating them as far back as the Miocene Epoch period. This was a period of time before any humans were alive.
column14: Great white sharks originated in the ocean in the Miocene Epoch period, they are evolved from shark species even bigger than them. The world was very different back then and the location of all the places in the world were different. That means great white sharks come from a part of the ocean that is not there anymore. Now they are found all over the ocean, anywhere in the ocean that has cool salt water. Some places you might find sharks today are the coasts of South Africa, California, Australia, and New Zealand.

column10: A turtle is an animal with four legs, a small tail, a head, and a shell on its back. Turtles can be found nearly all over the world and can live in many places—some like to swim in water, while others live on land. Tortoises' feet are similar shapes to elephants' feet for walking on land, and turtles' feet are webbed for swimming. If a turtle feels scared, it can pull its head and legs inside its shell to hide. Fossils show that turtles have been living on Earth for more than 200 million years! Turtles come in many sizes, shapes, and colors. Tortoises and turtles eat plants, but many turtles also eat a variety of other creatures including insects and fish.

column30: A sock is a soft piece of clothing a person wears on their foot. A sock often looks like a stretchy cloth tube or bag with a closed end for the toes and foot and an open end that pulls up to the ankle or leg. Socks protect the skin on feet by helping keep feet dry and a comfortable temperature. People make socks out of many materials and in many sizes and designs. People in Egypt and Greece used pieces of cloth or animal skin around their feet to make socks more than a thousand years ago.

column51: A vaccine is a special kind of medicine that helps keep people from getting sick. Usually a doctor or nurse will use a syringe with a small needle to give a person a vaccine shot in their arm or leg. Sometimes, it can also come as drops that you swallow. A vaccine, also called an immunization, helps your body learn what some bad germs called viruses or bacteria are like, so your body can practice and get better at fighting them. The first vaccine was made over 200 years ago by Edward Jenner in England to stop people from getting a disease called smallpox. Today, vaccines are used all over the world to help protect people from many different diseases and illnesses, and people are making new vaccines to protect against new diseases.
